data.title: CEX exchange reserves soar to $225B, as Bitget & MEXC see 262%+ growth. Institutional platforms witness asset outflow.
data.description: CoinGecko's report on centralized exchanges for 2026 shows exchange reserves totaling $225.4 billion. The report highlights significant growth of over 262% for Bitget and MEXC, contrasting with institutional platforms experiencing a loss in assets.

data.title: Dubai sets rules for token issuance, with new guidance categorizing launches into three buckets to ensure transparency. #RWAs #stablecoins #regulation
data.description: The regulatory authority in Dubai has released new guidelines categorizing token launches into three groups, while also enforcing stricter rules regarding transparency and governance for stablecoins, RWAs, and various digital assets. This move aims to improve oversight and accountability in the digital asset space, reflecting an increasing focus on ensuring compliance and risk management.

data.title: Anthony Scaramucci Flags Google's Quantum Crypto Warning As Mike Novogratz Assures Bitcoin, Ethereum Will Implement Quantum-Resistant Upgrades
data.description: Anthony Scaramucci, former White House communications director and founder of SkyBridge Capital, recently pointed out the potential risks quantum computing could pose to cryptocurrency, specifically mentioning Alphabet Inc.'s (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) Google. In a post on X, Scaramucci discussed Google's March 2026 report titled "Safeguarding cryptocurrency by disclosing quantum vulnerabilities responsibly," which highlighted the possibility of quantum computers breaking the cryptography that secures major digital currencies like Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) and Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) sooner than anticipated. Crypto investor Mike Novogratz also weighed in on the issue, expressing confidence in the industry's ability to adapt to such threats. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: CertifID Bolsters Executive Team with Two Senior Leaders Following $47.5M Series C
data.description: In Austin, Texas, and Grand Rapids, Michigan, CertifID, the top wire fraud protection platform for real estate, has named Josh Linn as Chief Product Officer and Liz Conn as Chief Marketing Officer. These appointments follow CertifID securing $47.5 million in Series C funding led by Centana Growth Partners. This move showcases a major growth in the company's leadership team in response to the changing landscape of wire fraud threats. CertifID has successfully prevented over $XX million in potential fraud.

data.title: Energy shock from the war with Iran has complicated Fed's decision-making on interest rates amid inflation concerns.
data.description: The Federal Reserve is facing additional complexity in determining interest rates due to an energy shock caused by ongoing conflicts. This unexpected development has added a new dimension to the decision-making process at the Federal Reserve.

data.title: Is "social media gorgeous" the key to the next food trend? Ube's popularity, experts say, is based on its vibrant color rather than taste or origin.
data.description: Experts attribute the rise in popularity of ube less to its Filipino roots or taste, and more to its vibrant color, stating that it's the hue that is driving its appeal.
